fields.description:
## Why

Static wiring tests cannot prove that an LLM criterion distinguishes concrete breakage scenarios from harmless silence or valid project customization. A balanced, repeatable evaluation is needed before the blocking project criterion is considered ready.

## What

Add a project criterion evaluation corpus, add structural tests for that corpus, run the existing live evaluation runner, and tune only the project rubric if calibration exposes false positives or missed portability failures.

## Scope

In scope:

- `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`
- `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`, only for calibration-driven rubric adjustments
- `tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py`
- live evaluation of `project.portability`

Out of scope:

- core registry, verifier, or disposition changes
- coaching move behavior
- existing portability debt
- adding requirements for client-agnostic wording

## Approach

Use the existing project eval override path proven by `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md`: `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml` resolves for logical criterion `project.portability`. Author this exact top-level contract:

- `prompt: plan-review-project-portability`
- `model: anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `epochs: 3`
- `gate: at_least(2)`
- `coverage_threshold: 1.0`
- one deterministic `emits_valid_findings` scorer
- a balanced `dataset` plus a non-empty `gold_set`

The dataset contains exactly eight ids. Each must-fire case uses `expect: finding`, includes an inline `input` plan, and records `expected_counterexample` metadata with non-empty `plan_citation`, `alternate_shape`, `causal_mechanism`, and `observable_breakage` fields:

- `PORT-F1`: reusable core adds `rebar landing` with a Gerrit-only push flow; alternate clients do not use this repository policy.
- `PORT-F2`: reusable core adds Python-only review criteria; Java, Next.js, .NET, Ruby, and Terraform projects cannot apply them.
- `PORT-F3`: reusable execution requires the macOS `security` binary; Windows, Linux, and BSD fail before work begins.
- `PORT-F4`: remote MCP derives the target repo from `Path.cwd()` plus unrestricted local filesystem access; a server outside the checkout cannot find or read the project.

Each must-not-fire case uses `expect: pass`:

- `PORT-N1`: the plan is silent about portability and contains no incompatible mechanism.
- `PORT-N2`: Gerrit-specific coaching remains in `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` project configuration.
- `PORT-N3`: an additive terminal-color feature is explicitly limited to the CLI and leaves library plus MCP behavior unchanged.
- `PORT-N4`: workspace discovery accepts an explicit path and uses portable standard-library filesystem operations.

The `gold_set` contains all eight cases. Each gold entry carries a non-empty `input` scenario description plus a matching `finding` or `pass` `label`. Run each dataset case three times through `rebar criteria eval`. The release threshold is recall `1.0`, false-accept rate `0.0`, `agreement: 1.0`, per-case `stability >= 0.6666666667`, and Cohen kappa `>= 0.70`. If the threshold is missed, revise only `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md` and rerun; do not change the generic registry, Pass 2, or Pass 3. If any model call times out, returns an API error, or produces a partial result, do not attest the partial output: discard that CLI result and rerun the complete command until all eight cases have exactly three completed epochs.

`tests/unit/test_criteria_eval.py` is the prior art for project prompt-id mapping, injected calibration, metric keys, and N-run stability. The new structural tests use the real eval loader plus `validate_eval_spec`; they use an injected solve function for deterministic metric assertions and never make a billable call.

## Dependencies

Depends on the criterion-integration child because calibration executes the activated project criterion and its authored rubric.

## Test Strategy

Validate corpus structure deterministically, then use the live criterion evaluation command for semantic calibration. Record the model, timestamp, run count, and aggregate metrics on this ticket so the external LLM result is auditable by the completion gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The project eval override resolves from `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k override_resolution`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `prompt: plan-review-project-portability`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `model: anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `epochs: 3` plus `gate: at_least(2)`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `coverage_threshold: 1.0`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares one deterministic `emits_valid_findings` scorer. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The dataset contains exactly `PORT-F1` through `PORT-F4` with `expect: finding`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k balanced_corpus`.
- [ ] The dataset contains exactly `PORT-N1` through `PORT-N4` with `expect: pass`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k balanced_corpus`.
- [ ] Every must-fire case defines non-empty plan-citation, alternate-shape, causal-mechanism, plus observable-breakage gold fields. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k finding_contract`.
- [ ] The must-not-fire cases respectively cover silence, project configuration, explicit surface scoping, plus portable alternatives. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k negative_boundaries`.
- [ ] Every gold entry carries a non-empty `input` plus the expected `label`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k gold_set`.
- [ ] An injected perfect solve returns the exact metric mapping specified in the Approach. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k calibration_metrics`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] One complete CLI result satisfies the exact live threshold mapping in the Approach; a ticket comment records the command, model, UTC timestamp, run count, aggregate metrics, plus every case result. Prove with `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json`.
- [ ] No calibration change touches generic implementation under `src/rebar/`. Prove with `test -z "$(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD -- src/rebar)"`.
- [ ] The final project rubric retains every structural marker specified by `immune-floury-toad`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py`.
